chunks.chunk_text: The default chunk size in SecondBrain is 4096 tokens. This is the standard configuration for document processing. You can configure the chunk size using the SECONDBRAIN_CHUNK_SIZE environment variable.
MongoDB connection URI configuration is done through the SECONDBRAIN_MONGO_URI environment variable. The default MongoDB URI is mongodb://localhost:27017 for local development.
Logging configuration uses SECONDBRAIN_LOG_LEVEL environment variable with values INFO, DEBUG, WARNING, or ERROR. The SECONDBRAIN_LOG_FORMAT controls output format with options pretty or json.
Circuit breaker protection can be enabled by setting SECONDBRAIN_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED=true. This provides automatic failure handling with recovery mechanisms for production reliability.
Rate limiting configuration uses SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true and SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS_PER_SECOND to control request throughput.
Structured logging configuration uses SECONDBRAIN_LOG_LEVEL and SECONDBRAIN_LOG_FORMAT environment variables for log output control.

SecondBrain supports multiple document formats for ingestion including PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown files, images, and audio files. The Ingestor class handles multi-format document parsing and automatic chunking.
Document processing pipeline includes parsing, chunking, embedding generation, and vector storage. Each document is processed independently and can be parallelized across multiple CPU cores.

PDF documents are extracted with text and layout preservation. DOCX files support Word document formatting. PPTX files extract presentation content. XLSX files process spreadsheet data with multiple sheets.

SecondBrain system architecture consists of five main components: CLI layer for user commands, Document Ingestor for parsing, Embedding Engine using OpenAI-compatible API, Storage Layer with MongoDB, and Searcher for vector search. Data flows from document ingestion through chunking, embedding generation, storage, and semantic search.

Circuit breaker pattern provides automatic failure handling with recovery mechanisms. When a service fails repeatedly, the circuit breaker opens and prevents further requests until the service recovers.
Rate limiting protects downstream services from overload by throttling request throughput. Configure with SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED and SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS_PER_SECOND.

Available SecondBrain CLI commands include: secondbrain ingest for document ingestion, secondbrain search for semantic queries, secondbrain chat for interactive conversations, secondbrain ls to list documents, secondbrain delete to remove documents, secondbrain status for database statistics, and secondbrain health for system checks. Use -v or --verbose flag for detailed output.
The Ingestor class handles multi-format document parsing and automatic chunking. It supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, images, and audio files with configurable chunk sizes and overlap.
Use the secondbrain search command with a natural language query to perform semantic search over your documents. The search returns the most relevant chunks ranked by cosine similarity.

Semantic search uses embedding vectors to find documents semantically similar to your query, not just keyword matches. This enables natural language queries like 'what is this about?' or 'main themes in this document'.
Search results can be customized with --top-k to control the number of results, --threshold to set minimum similarity score, and --fields to select which metadata fields to return.
Default model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2 from OpenAI-compatible API library. This model provides a good balance of speed and accuracy for most use cases.

Embedding models can be configured via SECONDBRAIN_LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL environment variable. Other supported models include all-mpnet-base-v2 for higher accuracy or paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2 for multilingual support.
Use SECONDBRAIN_CHUNK_SIZE and SECONDBRAIN_CHUNK_OVERLAP environment variables to configure chunk size and overlap. Default chunk size is 4096 tokens with 256 token overlap.
Chunk size affects retrieval quality and performance. Larger chunks provide more context but may reduce precision. Smaller chunks are more precise but may miss important context.
Chunk overlap preserves context between adjacent chunks. The default 256 token overlap ensures that important information at chunk boundaries is not lost.
Set SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true and configure requests per second with SECONDBRAIN_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS_PER_SECOND to enable rate limiting.
Rate limiting prevents overload of downstream services by throttling request throughput. Default is 10 requests per second when enabled.
Set SECONDBRAIN_LOG_LEVEL and SECONDBRAIN_LOG_FORMAT environment variables to configure structured logging. Log levels include DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.
Log format options include 'pretty' for human-readable output and 'json' for structured logging suitable for log aggregation systems.
Structured logging includes contextual information like request IDs, document IDs, and operation types for easier debugging and monitoring.
